Title: Neofunctionalized RGF pathways drive haustorial organogenesis in parasitic plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with prehaustorium development downstream of RGF signaling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLETHORA/AP2-domain transcription factor with two AP2 domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Protein accumulates/localizes in developing prehaustoria after DMBQ, host infection, or PjRGF treatment, indicating activation of downstream RGF pathway
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native protein localizes to RAM before induction; after DMBQ or host, localizes to RAM, transition zone, and prehaustorium; after PjRGF treatment also seen in elongated meristemic zone and prehaustorium
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, inferred from transcription factor identity and nuclear fluorescent signal
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Native promoter translational fusion imaging; transcriptional reporter; DMBQ and peptide treatment imaging
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ treatment; host infection; exogenous PjRGF1/PjRGF2/PjRGF5 treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a downstream signaling marker analogous to Arabidopsis PLT2 stabilization by RGF peptides; mechanistic role in P. japonicum prehaustorium not directly genetically tested in this study.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjPLT is the closest P. japonicum homolog of Arabidopsis PLT2. 2. PjPLT protein localizes to the prehaustorium after DMBQ, host infection, and PjRGF treatment. 3. The broader transcriptional reporter pattern versus more restricted protein pattern suggests posttranslational regulation downstream of RGF signaling.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Can promote prehaustorium formation when applied exogenously
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Root meristem growth factor (RGF/GLV/CLEL-type) sulfated secreted peptide hormone
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Exogenous peptide induces prehaustoria and PjYUC3 expression, but promoter is not prehaustorium-specific, suggesting a lesser or indirect endogenous role
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Prehaustorium apex epidermal auxin response domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated by DMBQ but not host exudate; promoter broadly expressed in epidermis throughout root and lateral roots, not specifically in prehaustorium
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; synthetic peptide treatment; promoter-reporter localization
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although bioactive when applied, the paper concludes PjRGF2 and PjRGF5 are the main prehaustorium-expressed RGF peptides, so PjRGF1 is not a primary endogenous haustorium signal.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGF1 was initially identified as a DMBQ-induced RGF gene. 2. Exogenous PjRGF1 can induce prehaustoria and PjYUC3 expression. 3. PjRGF1 promoter is broadly root-epidermal rather than prehaustorium-specific, so it likely plays a lesser role than PjRGF2/PjRGF5 in endogenous haustorial signaling.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds/responds to PjRGF2 and PjRGF5 in chimera assays but single knockout does not alter prehaustorium frequency
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter expressed from transition zone through root and asymmetrically in inner cortex/base of developing prehaustorium at 1 DPI
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-reporter localization; receptor chimera assay; CRISPR knockout phenotyping
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors suggest PjRGFR4 may function after the initial prehaustorium stage because its promoter localizes to the base of haustoria at 1 DPI; no clear single-mutant phenotype in early induction assay.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGFR4 promoter localizes to the developing prehaustorium. 2. PjRGFR4 responds to both PjRGF2 and PjRGF5 in chimera assays. 3. pjrgfr4 single knockout does not significantly alter prehaustorium formation, suggesting redundancy or later-stage function.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Possible supporting role in haustorial development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter shows some prehaustorium localization but single knockout does not significantly affect prehaustorium formation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongest in RAM and transition zone before induction; promoter shows some prehaustorium specificity at 1 DPT and asymmetry in inner cortex at 1 DPI
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite localized expression in the prehaustorium, genetic evidence in this study did not support a decisive nonredundant role in early prehaustorium formation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGFR5 is expressed in RAM/transition zone and localizes partly to the prehaustorium. 2. pjrgfr5 single knockout shows no significant prehaustorium phenotype. 3. The gene may function redundantly or in contexts not resolved by the assay.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Indole-3-pyruvate monooxygenase / YUCCA auxin biosynthesis enzyme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target metabolite through auxin biosynthetic step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes prehaustorium formation and haustorial development in the parasitic plant
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Prehaustorium apex epidermal region / root tip-associated auxin response domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulated downstream of PjRGF1, PjRGF2, and PjRGF5 and required for prehaustorium formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: required for haustorium formation; key control point stated for local auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in root tips and at the apex of the prehaustorium; induced 6 h after DMBQ or PjRGF treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR induction after peptide treatment; prior functional requirement cited by authors; DR5 auxin reporter localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ treatment; host root exudate/association; exogenous PjRGF peptide treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is not the main subject of experimentation in this paper but is repeatedly positioned as the downstream auxin biosynthesis node through which RGF signaling converges.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjYUC3 expression was induced by PjRGF1, PjRGF2, and PjRGF5. 2. The RGF-triggered prehaustorium pathway converges on PjYUC3-mediated auxin biosynthesis. 3. Prior work cited in this paper established PjYUC3 as required for prehaustorium formation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes prehaustorium formation and haustorial organogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Root meristem growth factor (RGF/GLV/CLEL-type) sulfated secreted peptide hormone
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand-mediated activation of receptor kinase signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis-linked developmental signaling for prehaustorium formation; regulation of cell division in developing prehaustorium
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential haustorium-specific endogenous inducer acting redundantly with PjRGF5; strongest peptide activity among tested PjRGFs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: DMBQ; host root exudate from Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; synthetic peptide treatment; promoter-reporter localization; receptor chimera assay; CRISPR knockout; phenotyping of prehaustorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ treatment; host exudate; exogenous sulfated/unsulfated peptide application
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces prehaustoria, induces PjYUC3, and regulates later cell division patterning in the prehaustorium
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Prehaustorium apex epidermal auxin response domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated by DMBQ and host exudate; promoter active in transition zone early and in prehaustorium, strongest at apex after DMBQ and central/base region at 1 DPI with host
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Signals through RGFR receptor kinase complexes, exact complex composition not established here
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High peptide concentrations can trigger nonspecific epidermal/cortical cell division
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to disrupt parasitic haustorium initiation or engineer anti-parasitic strategies by blocking peptide signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PjRGF2 is broadly conserved in Orobanchaceae and likely represents an ancient haustorium-related RGF peptide; single knockout did not reduce prehaustorium number, consistent with redundancy, but increased abnormal pointed prehaustoria.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGF2 is induced by DMBQ and host exudate. 2. Exogenous PjRGF2 induces prehaustoria and PjYUC3 expression. 3. PjRGF2 promoter is active in the prehaustorium. 4. PjRGF2 activates PjRGFR1/3/4 in chimera assays. 5. pjrgf2 knockout alters later prehaustorial cell division but not overall prehaustorium frequency, indicating redundancy.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes prehaustorium formation and haustorial organogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Root meristem growth factor (RGF/GLV/CLEL-type) sulfated secreted peptide hormone
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand-mediated activation of receptor kinase signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis-linked developmental signaling for prehaustorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Haustorium-specific endogenous inducer acting redundantly with PjRGF2; neofunctionalized duplicated peptide family
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: DMBQ; host root exudate from Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; synthetic peptide treatment; promoter-reporter localization; receptor chimera assay; genomic duplication analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ treatment; host exudate; exogenous peptide application
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces prehaustoria and PjYUC3 expression; contributes to haustorium-specific signaling likely redundantly
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Prehaustorium apex epidermal auxin response domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by DMBQ and host exudate; promoter expressed in epidermal cells of DMBQ- and host-induced prehaustoria; increased in transition zone early after DMBQ
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Signals through RGFR receptor kinase complexes, exact complex composition not established here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-parasitic target, though multiple duplicated copies complicate gene editing
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PjRGF5 underwent segmental tandem duplication, producing multiple viable copies and pseudogenes; authors infer recent innovation/neofunctionalization in P. japonicum. A direct knockout phenotype was not tested due to copy number complexity.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGF5 is induced by DMBQ and host exudate. 2. Exogenous PjRGF5 induces prehaustoria and PjYUC3 expression. 3. PjRGF5 promoter localizes to epidermal cells of the prehaustorium. 4. PjRGF5 activates PjRGFR3/4 in chimera assays. 5. PjRGF5 appears to be a recently duplicated and neofunctionalized haustorium-related peptide.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports prehaustorium formation and haustorial organogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Peptide perception and receptor-mediated signaling activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis-linked developmental signaling for prehaustorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of prehaustorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts upstream of PjYUC3-mediated auxin biosynthesis via RGF signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-reporter localization; ligand-receptor chimera assay; CRISPR knockout phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ treatment; host interaction; exogenous peptide context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout significantly reduces DMBQ-induced prehaustorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter expressed throughout root and localizes asymmetrically in prehaustorium at 1 DPT/DPI
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely receptor complex typical of RGFR signaling; specific co-receptors not tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate target for suppressing parasitic prehaustorium initiation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activated by PjRGF2 but not PjRGF5 in the BRI1 chimera assay; functionally redundant with PjRGFR3 for prehaustorium formation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGFR1 promoter is expressed in roots and prehaustoria. 2. PjRGFR1 responds to PjRGF2 in the chimera assay. 3. pjrgfr1 knockout significantly reduces prehaustorium formation. 4. PjRGFR1 is therefore a crucial positive regulator of haustorial signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports prehaustorium formation and haustorial organogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Peptide perception and receptor-mediated signaling activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis-linked developmental signaling for prehaustorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator integrating both PjRGF2 and PjRGF5 signals
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts upstream of PjYUC3-mediated auxin biosynthesis via RGF signaling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: May be transcriptionally down-regulated after DMBQ treatment, possibly downstream of RGF signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter-reporter localization; ligand-receptor chimera assay; CRISPR knockout phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ treatment; host interaction; exogenous peptide context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout significantly reduces DMBQ-induced prehaustorium formation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter expressed in inner cortex of root; transcript decreased after DMBQ treatment; promoter not obviously localized to prehaustorium despite functional importance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely receptor complex typical of RGFR signaling; specific co-receptors not tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate target for suppressing parasitic prehaustorium initiation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally resembles an immunity-related RGFR clade member co-opted for haustorial development according to authors.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGFR3 transcript decreases after DMBQ treatment. 2. PjRGFR3 is activated by both PjRGF2 and PjRGF5 in chimera assays. 3. pjrgfr3 knockout significantly reduces prehaustorium formation. 4. PjRGFR3 is a key positive regulator of haustorium developmental signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects proper prehaustorial morphogenesis linked to auxin-associated development
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cell division patterning during later prehaustorium development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-specific controller of prehaustorial cell division rather than main inducer frequency
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Likely other redundant PjRGF peptides; not activated by PjRGF2 or PjRGF5 in assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout increases frequency of aberrant pointed prehaustoria, indicating regulation of cell division at the apex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter expressed in epidermis and in epidermal cells of prehaustorium at 1 DPT and 1 DPI
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely receptor complex typical of RGFR signaling; specific co-receptors not tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential secondary target to disrupt proper haustorial morphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Genetically implicated in later prehaustorial cell division but not bound by PjRGF2 or PjRGF5 in the in planta chimera assay, suggesting additional unidentified ligands.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PjRGFR2 promoter localizes to epidermal cells including the prehaustorium. 2. pjrgfr2 knockout does not reduce prehaustorium frequency. 3. pjrgfr2 knockout increases abnormal pointed prehaustoria, implicating PjRGFR2 in cell division control at later stages.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes prehaustorium formation in a parasitic plant
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Root meristem growth factor (RGF/GLV/CLEL-type) sulfated secreted peptide hormone
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports conservation of haustorium-related RGF signaling across parasitic Orobanchaceae
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; synthetic peptide application and phenotype assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ShRGF2 is DMBQ-inducible and exogenous peptide induces prehaustoria in S. hermonthica
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential broad-spectrum anti-parasitic target across Orobanchaceae
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as supportive cross-species evidence; not mechanistically dissected as deeply as P. japonicum genes.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShRGF2 is homologous to PjRGF2. 2. ShRGF2 is induced by DMBQ. 3. Exogenous ShRGF2 induces prehaustoria in S. hermonthica, supporting evolutionary conservation of RGF-mediated haustorial signaling.
